name: Tommy Flanagan
abstract: Thomas Lee Flanagan (March 16, 1930 – November 16, 2001) was an American jazz pianist and composer. He grew up in Detroit, initially influenced by such pianists as Art Tatum, Teddy Wilson, and Nat King Cole, and then by bebop musicians.

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: My favorite record is one that I made many, many years ago called Art. It was on the Argo label with Tommy Flanagan playing the piano...
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: Tommy and I, Tommy Flanagan and I were best friends, musically speaking but also socially.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: So I had Art Farmer, had Tommy Flanagan. I had Ron and Don Thompson, as well. ... So I got him over from Bermuda to play on a couple tracks of the Commitment album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Tommy Flanagan, Kenny Burrell, Pepper Adams... Kenny Burrell grabbed me and put me in that because I was a Westside guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: I got Tommy Potter and Tommy Flanagan and Elvin Jones... And then Roland Hanna, he came in after Tommy Flanagan and played with me.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Locke
about_quotes.evidence: The things they did with that quartet that we had with Major Holly, Tommy Flanagan, myself... we must have made about ten albums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Locke interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, NY, January 13, 2001
